### Post by astoltz on 2007-12-14
I know it's not really an answer to your question, but why have you installed Beryl?  It's no longer being developed and instead has been folded back into compiz.  The combined efforts are now comiz-fusion and that is installed by default in Gutsy (I'm assuming you installed Gutsy).

---

### Post by GujuLvr on 2007-12-14
yeaa i got 7.10 yea i installed beryl

### Post by flamelab on 2007-12-14
Well go to Synaptic and uninstall EVERYTHING related to Beryl (that has its code name )
Then install the nvidia driver through the Restricted Drivers Manager or Synaptic and then restart Xorg .
After that you have Compiz Fusion activated by default . 
Install 

```
sudo aptitude install compizconfig-settings-manager
```
(you now have a control panel for Compiz Fusion )

and you have finished !:guitar:

### Post by flamelab on 2007-12-14
You mean ???:confused:

You had to do 

1)```
sudo apt-get install linux-restricted-modules-generic
```
2)```
Go to System > Administration > Restricted Drivers Manager and turn on the driver
```
3)From Software Sources check all boxes , reload apt-get (or synaptic ) in order to rebuild the cache 
and
4)install **nvidia-glx-new**

### Post by GujuLvr on 2007-12-15
thankx but this was the problem
Under Ubuntu Feisty Fawn 7.04, compiz is the default desktop effect window manager.
When first enabled by using desktop-effects, there used to 4 desktop by default. After disabling and reenabling it, only one virtual desktop was left, therefore the cube plugin of compiz was not working anymore.

In order to make the cube plugins of compiz to work, the desktop needs to have more than "1" face to rotate.

Somewhow, after disabling and reenabling compiz, the desktop number changed to 1...

In order to get this number back to something like 4, you need to start gconf-editor, to start it, type the hotkey Alt+F2 and type gconf-editor, then go to /apps/compiz/general/screen0/options/ and change the value of hsize from 1 to 4.

People using the command line can use the following command:

```
$ gconftool -s /apps/compiz/general/screen0/options/hsize --type int 4
```

This will set up the number of desktop in your session.

founD it online... somehwere.
